name: Slurm Output Log Triage
summary: Collect read-only evidence for missing or confusing Slurm output logs.
description: This skill helps users and support teams diagnose missing, empty, misplaced, or confusing Slurm stdout/stderr logs by collecting accounting, job-record, output-path, working-directory, and filesystem evidence without modifying job or user data.

tools.name: sacct
tools.notes: Optional accounting evidence for StdOut, StdErr, SubmitLine, state, and exit code when available.

tools.name: scontrol
tools.notes: Optional job-record evidence for WorkDir, StdOut, StdErr, and command details.

tools.name: squeue
tools.notes: Optional current job-state evidence when the job is still visible.

inputs.name: job_id
inputs.description: Slurm job id, array task id, or job step id to inspect.

inputs.name: log_file
inputs.description: Optional known stdout, stderr, or application log path to inspect.

inputs.name: error_file
inputs.description: Optional separate stderr path to inspect when stdout and stderr were split.

inputs.name: report_dir
inputs.description: Directory where the read-only evidence bundle should be written.

outputs.name: accounting_report
outputs.description: Slurm sacct table with state, exit code, submit/start/end times, stdout/stderr filename patterns, and submission line.

outputs.name: job_record
outputs.description: Optional scontrol job record with WorkDir, StdOut, StdErr, Command, and related job metadata.

outputs.name: path_checks
outputs.description: Read-only path existence, file size, permissions, and tail evidence for supplied and default output candidates.

outputs.name: log_signals
outputs.description: Matching lines for output, path, permission, module, command-not-found, quota, and filesystem symptoms.

outputs.name: triage_summary
outputs.description: Human-readable prompts for deciding whether logs are in a different working directory, were empty, failed before launch, or need clearer sbatch output directives.

examples.title: Review missing or empty output logs

tests.description: Validate the manifest and example files.
Check shell syntax for the evidence collection script.
Run against a Slurm job with missing, empty, or unexpected output logs and confirm the report stays read-only.
